---
title: "Configuring The Cic Document Upload Feature"
---

---
title: "Configuring the CIC Document Upload Feature"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-for-Guidewire-ClaimCenter/vwh1756484606438"
section: "Hyland for Guidewire ClaimCenter"
---

The CIC Document Upload feature is configured using Hyland Automate. See the Hyland Automate Administration for general information on configuring document upload.

You will need to contact Guidewire Cloud Operations and request an InsuranceSuite REST API registration with Guidewire Hub using service with service account mapping auth flow. Guidewire will provide you with a Client ID, Client Secret, scope, and Auth Server URI. These values are required as you configure your solution for OAuth 2.0.Note: If the Auth Server URI provided by Guidewire does not end in /v1/token, manually add /v1/token to the URI. The modified URI is the value you will need for the following procedures.
To configure CIC Document Upload for your Hyland for Guidewire ClaimCenter solution:

Choose one of the following:
- For a new deployment:
1. In the Studio Admin application, navigate to Project Releases and locate the CIC Document Upload project.
2. Click the vertical ellipsis to the right of the project. ![](data:image/gif;base64,) ![A screenshot of the vertical ellipsis button]()
3. Select Deploy. The Deploy Application wizard is displayed.
4. Perform project deployment as normal until the Authentications page is displayed. See the Hyland Automate Administration for general information on deploying a project.
5. On the Authentications page, enter the Client ID provided by Guidewire in the Client id field.
6. Enter the Client Secret provided by Guidewire in the Client secret field.
7. Enter the Auth Server URI provided by Guidewire in the Endpoint field. Note: If the Auth Server URI provided by Guidewire does not end in /v1/token, manually add /v1/token to the URI.
8. Enter the scope values provided by Guidewire in the Scope field, using a space to separate each individual scope.
9. Click Next and continue project deployment as normal. See the Hyland Automate Administration for general information on deploying a project.
- For an existing deployment:
1. In the Studio Admin application, navigate to Application Instances and locate the CIC Document Upload application.
2. Click the vertical ellipsis to the right of the application. ![](data:image/gif;base64,) ![A screenshot of the vertical ellipsis button]()
3. Select Upgrade. The Upgrade wizard is displayed.
4. Select the Authentications tab.
5. Enter the Client ID provided by Guidewire in the Client id field.
6. Enter the Client Secret provided by Guidewire in the Client secret field.
7. Enter the Auth Server URI provided by Guidewire in the Endpoint field. Note: If the Auth Server URI provided by Guidewire does not end in /v1/token, manually add /v1/token to the URI.
8. Enter the scope values provided by Guidewire in the Scope field, using a space to separate each individual scope.
9. Configure any other details as needed, then click Upgrade.
